sentences.english: Hello. This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.

sentences.english: I'm Neil.
And I'm Beth.
Nowadays, it's popular to find out more about your family history by tracing your family tree – a diagram that shows all the members of a family over a long period of time.

sentences.english: Well, my Mum actually did our family tree, and she found a lot of our ancestors had emigrated to the United States.

sentences.english: But, yeah, I don't know much more than that.
What about you?
Well, I'm lucky to have an unusual surname, so it's quite easy to trace my ancestors and somebody in my family has gone back to 1700 and found somebody with my name.
sentences.korean: 드문 성을 가져서 운이 좋아요. 조상을 추적하기가 꽤 쉽거든요.

sentences.english: Wow, that's amazing. Well, the internet has made it easier to find the historical records needed to research your ancestry - the origins of your family.
sentences.korean: 와, 대단하네요. 인터넷이 가문을 조사하는 데 필요한 역사적 기록을 찾기 쉽게 만들었어요.

sentences.english: But what if you want to find out about a time before historical records began?

sentences.english: Luckily, some companies now sell DNA ancestry kits which claim to reveal the countries of origin in your DNA.

sentences.english: But just how accurate are the results from these DNA tests?

sentences.english: Here he describes the reaction to his first DNA test:
When I first got back my ancestry test from Company 1, it showed that I was 70% English, 15% German, which was really cool.

sentences.english: I have family from the north of England, so you might expect those kind of Nordic and northern connections.

But the second company's results were very different.
They tested the same DNA but found something surprising — 27.5% Irish, Scottish and Welsh, but explicitly not English.

theme: Can you trust ancestry DNA kits?

sentences.english: So Greg's results were completely contradictory depending on which company he used.

sentences.english: Actually, companies compare DNA with other samples in their database, and since companies use different databases, it's not too surprising that Greg's results were so different – unlike his actual DNA.

sentences.english: The answer is b) 99.9%.
